pages.full_text: Tympanum, Cormac's chapel, Rock of Cashel.
photo_captions: Tympanum, Cormac's chapel, Rock of Cashel.
pages.notes: Page is dominated by a large colour photograph of the tympanum of Cormac's Chapel, Rock of Cashel. The caption is handwritten vertically beside the photo. Partial words visible at the right margin are gutter bleed-through from the right page and have not been re-transcribed here.

pages.full_text:
Rock of Cashel

[hand-drawn sketch of Rock of Cashel skyline]

Town below

[hand-drawn sketch of a building with a town below]

* * * * *

7.15. pm we arrived at __Vale of Aherlow__. The campsite house + restaurant is in the stables of a disappeared [?C] house. There is a huge, deserted, walled garden nearby. Pets, pingpong, pool. John likes this site even more than the last. Lots of hills nearby, including Galtree. Supper in the restaurant.

Thu. Rain poured in the night + as we left. Damp towels dried out as we drove along: lunch in a lush field of clover with a beautiful view of wooded valley. V. fertile. We shopped in __Mitchel town__.
pages.route_sketch_description: Two hand-drawn pencil sketches: upper sketch shows the skyline of the Rock of Cashel (castle/cathedral complex with round tower), labelled 'Rock of Cashel' top right; lower sketch shows a two-storey building representing the town below, labelled 'Town below' to the right.
pages.notes: Page number '13' visible in red/pink ink top-right corner. The word 'disappeared' is followed by what may be an abbreviated word in a circle — reads like a circled 'C' or abbreviation, possibly 'C[ountry]' house; transcribed as [?C]. 'Mitchel town' appears as two words underlined; could be 'Mitchelstown' (a town in Co. Tipperary, Ireland). 'Galtee' (the Galtee Mountains) may be what is written as 'Galtree' — the diary spelling is preserved. The trip appears to be in Ireland (Rock of Cashel is in Co. Tipperary). No year directly visible on this page; volume covers 1989–2003.
